title: Staff dress code requirements
content: Staff members are required to wear business casual attire during school hours. They must avoid overly casual or inappropriate clothing, including jeans, flip flops, and tank tops.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

title: Student Uniform Requirements
content: Students must wear school uniforms, which consist of a white shirt, navy blue trousers or skirts, and black shoes.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

title: Uniform Neatness Standard
content: Student uniforms should always be neat and tidy, reflecting the commitment to maintaining high standards of appearance.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

title: Excessive accessories use in educational setting
content: Excessive or distracting accessories, including large jewelry or brightly colored headbands, are discouraged for both students and staff to maintain focus within the educational setting.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

title: Non-compliance with the dress code
content: Non-compliance with the dress code by students or staff may result in warnings, parental notification, or other disciplinary actions.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

title: Approach to discipline for dress code infractions
content: Our school emphasizes corrective measures that focus on learning and growth rather than punishment for dress code infractions.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

title: Dress Code Policy Rationale
content: At Imagine Montessori School Valencia, fostering a respectful and professional environment is a core value. The dress code reflects the values of discipline, self-respect, and equality, aiming to create a positive atmosphere conducive to learning and personal growth.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff

title: Acceptable clothing for staff
content: Acceptable clothing for staff includes knee-length skirts and dresses, blouses, smart t-shirts, polo shirts, trousers, smart jeans, and chinos.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

title: Inappropriate short clothing
content: Staff members are not permitted to wear mini-skirts, hot pants, or lycra cycling shorts that are shorter than mid-thigh at work.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

title: Leisure and sportswear
content: Staff members must not wear leisure shorts, tracksuits, or sportswear unless they are participating in Physical Education or related activities.

title: Inappropriate tops
content: Staff members are prohibited from wearing transparent or see-through blouses, dresses, or shirts. Additionally, low-cut tops, crop-tops, and vest or spaghetti-strap tops are not allowed.

Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.5. Staff Dress Code

title: Damaged clothing and offensive graphics
content: Staff members must avoid wearing clothing with tears, holes, or rips and should not wear clothes that bear offensive logos, slogans, or graphics.

title: Headwear and inappropriate shoes indoors
content: Staff members are not allowed to wear baseball caps, hats, high-heeled stiletto shoes, or beach flip-flops indoors during work hours.

title: Clothing safety
content: Staff members should avoid wearing clothes that restrict movement or can snag on equipment, such as clothes with loose pockets, sequins, or large buttons.

title: Proper wearing of underwear
content: Staff members must ensure that their underwear is not worn in a manner that is revealing or inappropriate during work hours.

title: Outdoor shoe requirement for students
content: Students must wear flat, enclosed shoes outdoors. These shoes should be comfortable and fastened with laces, buckles, zips, or velcro. Students must be able to put them on and take them off independently.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance
Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Indoor shoe requirement for students
content: Students must wear closed-toe shoes indoors to ensure safe walking. Bulky, furry, or flip-flop styles are not allowed. Anti-slip socks are also recommended.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance
Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Wearing hats during playground time
content: Students are permitted to wear hats only during playground time to protect from the sun.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance
Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance
Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Religious headwear permissions
content: Students may wear religious headwear at all times, provided that it does not obscure their vision. During PE lessons, religious headwear must be removed for health and safety reasons.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance
Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance
Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Hair length and style
content: Students may have any length and style of hair, but during PE lessons, any long hair must be tied back for safety reasons.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance
Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance
Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Student Dress Code
content: Students must wear appropriate sports underwear, shorts or leggings or tracksuit bottoms, a t-shirt or sweatshirt, and trainers during school activities.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Student Dress Code Policy
content: The Student Dress Code Policy requires all students to follow the specified dress code guidelines to promote an inclusive school culture.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Respect for Dress Code Guidelines
content: The Student Dress Code Policy mandates that all members of the school community respect the established dress code guidelines to support a welcoming and inclusive environment.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Jewellery Restrictions
content: Students must not wear jewellery that dangles, including hoop earrings and large necklaces, to ensure health and safety. On physical education (PE) days, students must be able to remove stud earrings or other items as needed.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Make-Up Guidelines
content: Students from Year 7 onwards may wear subtle make-up, such as mascara, powder, foundation, and nail varnish. Lipstick is not allowed.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Acrylic Nail Length
content: Students' acrylic nails must not extend more than two millimetres from the fingertip, for health and safety reasons.
source_spans: Dress Code Policy — Students and Staff > 2.4. Student Dress Code — General Guidance

title: Shorts and Skirts Length
content: Students' shorts and skirts must cover the upper thighs, requiring them to be ¾ length or longer. On PE days, cycling shorts are allowed but must also adhere to this length requirement.

title: Clothing Condition
content: Students must wear clothes without holes or rips and ensure that underwear is covered by outerwear at all times.
